example_failures.question: What is the total revenue from completed payments?
example_failures.generated_sql: SELECT SUM(amount) FROM payments WHERE status = 'completed'
example_failures.gold_sql: SELECT SUM(amount) FROM payments WHERE status='paid';

example_failures.question: Which 5 product categories generated the most revenue from completed orders?
example_failures.generated_sql:
SELECT category, SUM(line_total) AS total_revenue
FROM order_items
JOIN orders ON order_items.order_id = orders.order_id
JOIN payments ON orders.order_id = payments.order_id
WHERE orders.status = 'completed'
GROUP BY category
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 5
example_failures.gold_sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.line_total) rev FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id=o.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id=p.product_id WHERE o.status='completed' GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY rev DESC LIMIT 5;
example_failures.reason:
Generated SQL failed to execute: column "category" does not exist
LINE 1: SELECT category, SUM(line_total) AS total_revenue
               ^

results.question: What are the top 5 best-selling products by quantity in completed orders?
results.gold_sql: SELECT p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) q FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id=o.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id=p.product_id WHERE o.status='completed' GROUP BY p.product_id ORDER BY q DESC LIMIT 5;
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM order_items oi
JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
JOIN payments p ON o.order_id = p.order_id
JOIN products pr ON oi.product_id = pr.product_id
WHERE o.status = 'completed'
GROUP BY p.product_name
ORDER BY total_quantity DESC
LIMIT 5
results.reason:
Generated SQL failed to execute: column p.product_name does not exist
LINE 1: SELECT p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity
               ^
HINT:  Perhaps you meant to reference the column "pr.product_name".

results.question: How much revenue came from Gold-tier customers (completed payments)?
results.gold_sql: SELECT SUM(pm.amount) FROM payments pm JOIN orders o ON pm.order_id=o.order_id JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE pm.status='paid' AND c.tier='Gold';
results.generated_sql: SELECT SUM(line_total) FROM order_items AS oi INNER JOIN orders AS o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id INNER JOIN payments AS p ON o.order_id = p.order_id INNER JOIN customers AS c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE c.tier = 'Gold' AND p.status = 'completed'
results.reason: No column alignment produces a matching set of rows
